rationale: Only 2.7 years of experience with QA/test automation at Wayne Enterprises (fictional company honeypot), self-learner AI side projects only, and no production IR, ranking, or embedding system work despite listing Fine-tuning LLMs as advanced skill.
Entirely non-technical career spanning content writing, customer support, project management, and sales with no engineering or ML work whatsoever, despite listing Time Series as an advanced skill.

7 years split between IT services firms (Mindtree, TCS) doing Android/mobile dev and a fictional company (Stark Industries) doing DevOps, with OpenSearch listed as advanced but no described production IR or ranking experience.

Civil Engineer at Initech (fictional company honeypot) with an accounting role description and generic marketing manager summary copy-paste, representing a complete non-fit with zero technical or AI/ML relevance.

3.1 years at HCL (IT services) doing data engineering with lightweight ML (sklearn/XGBoost, ~30% of time) and pgvector exposure, but no production retrieval or ranking system ownership and entirely IT-services background.

12.2 years of non-technical roles (sales, accounting, mechanical engineering, business analyst) spanning multiple fictional companies (Stark Industries, Pied Piper, Initech), with no engineering or ML experience whatsoever.
9.3-year career entirely in HR management and business analyst roles at Infosys, Wayne Enterprises, and Wipro with no technical engineering work, despite listing Apache Beam and Databricks as skills.
Operations manager at Dunder Mifflin (fictional company honeypot) with a career spanning content writing, sales, and graphic design — a complete non-fit with no technical or ML work.
